vision_tagline: Your coding sessions are videos you can scrub, fork, and rewind
user_story: A developer finishes a long session where the AI made 47 file edits across 12 files with 3 agent switches. They want to understand WHY a test started failing. They open the Session Timeline panel, see a waveform of activity (file edits spike, tool calls cluster), and scrub backward through the session like a video editor — each frame shows the AI's thought, the tool it called, the diff it produced. They find the exact moment: a 'replace_symbol' tool changed a function signature. They fork the session at that point, type 'undo this change and fix the callers', and the AI branches from that exact context. No re-prompting, no context loss. The original session continues untouched. Later, they export the timeline as a replay link for a teammate: 'Watch what happened between 14:32 and 14:47'.
core_innovation: Session-as-video metaphor — continuous capture of every AI action + thought + tool output, indexed as frames on a scrubbable waveform, with fork-at-frame and replay-export capabilities

components.name: TimelineWaveform
components.description: D3-powered waveform visualization of session activity density. X-axis is time. Y-axis shows activity intensity (file edits, tool calls, LLM turns) as stacked colored bands. Renders at 60fps with windowed rendering for sessions with 10,000+ events. Supports click-to-scrub and shift+drag region select.

components.name: TimelineScrubber
components.description: A draggable playhead that sits on the waveform. Shows a live preview card as you scrub: current message snippet, tool name, file being edited. Supports keyboard nudge (left/right 500ms, shift+left/right 5s). Jumps to exact time on click.

components.name: TimelineFrameView
components.description: The main detail view when scrubbing. Shows a frozen snapshot of what the session looked like at that moment: the message being processed, the pending diff, the AI's current reasoning. Stacks tool calls in a vertical accordion. Each frame is a shallow clone of the session state at that instant.

components.name: ForkDialog
components.description: Modal that opens when user forks from a timeline point. Pre-fills the context from the forked moment (last user message, AI state, file snapshots). Shows diff of what will be preserved vs. lost. Let user type a new message and confirms fork.

components.name: TimelineReplayExport
components.description: Generates a shareable JSON replay file (or server-hosted link) that a teammate can load into their own OpenCode Desktop to replay the session frame-by-frame. Includes an optional voice-narrated walkthrough layer.

components.name: AnimatedFrameTransition
components.description: When the user scrubs or auto-plays a timeline, this component smoothly transitions between frames with a morph animation: diffs flow in/out, messages scroll-left/right, tool states animate. Gives the feeling of moving through time.

data_models.name: TimelineEvent
data_models.description: Single point on the timeline. Captures a continuous event (tool call start/end, LLM turn, file edit, permission granted). Stored in a sorted array indexed by timestamp.

schema.kind: tool_call | llm_turn | file_edit | permission | agent_switch | message

data_models.name: TimelineFrame
data_models.description: Snapshot of session state at a given timestamp. Reconstructed from TimelineEvent[] by replaying events up to that point.

data_models.name: TimelineBookmark
data_models.description: User-created bookmark on the timeline for later reference. Can have tags and notes.

integration_points.description: Extend SessionVizSession with TimelineEvent[] storage. Add 'recordEvent' method that the existing handler functions (handleToolCalled, handleFileEdited, etc.) call to append timeline events instead of just recent_activity.
Add TimelineRow variant for timeline bookmark and fork markers that appear inline in the message view.
Add 'Timeline' tab alongside existing 'Viz' and 'Review' tabs. The timeline panel replaces the side panel content when active.
Add IPC handler 'export-timeline-replay' that serializes a session's TimelineEvent[] to a file via dialog.showSaveDialog.
Use persisted store pattern (already established) for TimelineBookmark[] persistence so bookmarks survive app restarts.
Session SDK already emits Event objects. Add 'timeline_event' event type. No breaking change — it's additive alongside existing session events.

editor_experience: The timeline sits in the existing right side panel as a new tab ('Timeline') alongside 'Viz' and 'Review'. From any session view, click the tab to see the waveform. Scrub with mouse drag or keyboard arrows. A floating preview card follows the playhead. Keyboard shortcut Cmd+Shift+T toggles the timeline panel. Forking opens a dialog pre-filled with context from that moment. The timeline auto-updates in real-time during active sessions — you can watch the waveform grow as the AI works.
mobile_adaptation: Timeline is view-only on mobile. No scrub or fork. Shows a compact waveform with tap-to-jump functionality. Bookmark viewing and sharing links work fully.
key_technical_risks: Memory: sessions with 50,000+ events need windowed rendering. Use virtua patterns (already used in message-timeline.tsx) to render only visible timeline segments.

Replay fidelity: reconstructing exact session state at arbitrary timestamps requires deterministic event replay. Mitigation: store immutable event log, replay with fast-forward skip for non-visual events.
Diff storage: file diffs at each frame could be large. Mitigation: store diffs lazily (compress to SHA256, store full only on user bookmark/fork).

vision_tagline: Every surface is a command. Chat, palette, search — fused into one AI-native intent engine
user_story: A developer presses Cmd+K. Instead of a traditional command palette, they see a single text input with a glowing cursor. They type 'deploy the checkout fix to staging' — the AI parses intent, shows a confidence indicator ('Deploy checkout fix → staging: 94%'), runs the deploy pipeline, and surfaces the result. No app switching, no command discovery. Next, they type 'what was that weird error from yesterday' and the AI searches session history, finds the error, explains what caused it, and offers to write a regression test. Later, 'show me files touched by the auth PR' renders a file tree filtered by PR #42. The interface learns: frequently typed commands get one-key suggestions. The palette never shows a list of commands — it shows predictions. The user never 'discovers' a feature; they just describe what they want.
core_innovation: Eliminate the command list entirely. Replace discovery with intent parsing. The palette is a single input that understands natural language across chat, search, navigation, settings, tools, and AI actions.

components.name: UniversalInput
components.description: The single text input that replaces command palette, search bar, and chat input. Uses inline autocomplete (ghost text) for predicted actions. Shows confidence meter on the right. Supports natural language, fuzzy commands, file paths, and direct commands with equal weight. Renders below the cursor like Spotlight, not a centered modal.

components.name: IntentPreview
components.description: Shows below the UniversalInput as user types. Displays parsed intent, confidence score, and action preview. Updates live as input changes. Can show multiple interpretations ranked by confidence. Shows estimated effect ('Will edit 3 files') before execution.

components.name: ResultCanvas
components.description: Where the UniversalInput's result renders. Not a fixed panel — it adapts to the result type: chat response renders as a message, command output as a terminal block, file search as an interactive tree, session search as a timeline preview. Supports streaming updates for long-running actions.

components.name: ContextHintBar
components.description: Shows above the UniversalInput as a horizontal list of contextual suggestions. Example: when on a session page, shows 'Fork this session', 'Summarize changes', 'Find similar bugs'. When on settings, shows 'Change theme to...', 'Reset all settings'. Changes immediately as context changes.

components.name: ConfidenceMeter
components.description: Small animated bar beside the UniversalInput showing how confident the AI is in its parsed intent. Green = high confidence (execute directly). Yellow = medium (show preview). Red = low (ask clarifying question). Pulses while parsing.

components.name: ActionHistoryDrawer
components.description: Pull-up drawer from the bottom showing recent UniversalInput actions with their results. Acts as a unified history of everything the user asked the palette to do. Filterable by type (chat, command, search, action). Supports replay and undo.

data_models.name: IntentPayload
data_models.description: The parsed user intent — what the UniversalInput understands you want to do.

schema.intent_type: chat | command | search | navigate | action | deploy

data_models.name: ContextHint
data_models.description: Contextual suggestion that appears in the ContextHintBar.

data_models.name: ActionHistoryEntry
data_models.description: Record of a past UniversalInput action.

integration_points.description: Extend CommandProvider to emit IntentPayload. The existing command registration system (register function) already has a catalog — add 'intent_matchers' field to CommandRegistration that maps natural language patterns to command IDs. The existing keybind system carriers over unchanged.

Rewrite from list-based palette to UniversalInput-based intent parser. The existing palette infrastructure (Dialog + TextField + filtered list) becomes the UniversalInput + IntentPreview + ResultCanvas combination.

Add intent resolution route. When an IntentPayload with intent_type='chat' is submitted, route to existing prompt context for session message. For 'action' and 'command' types, route to tool execution or settings mutation.
Add 'resolve_intent' SDK method that sends user input to the AI server for intent parsing. Returns RankedIntent[]. Existing session event streaming carries the result execution.
Persist ActionHistoryEntry[] with the existing persisted() utility so history survives restarts.

editor_experience: Cmd+K opens the UniversalInput at the bottom of the window (not center) — it slides up like macOS Spotlight. The user starts typing. The IntentPreview shows below with confidence meter on the right. ContextHintBar above shows 3-4 contextual suggestions. Press Enter to execute the top intent. Tab to cycle through alternatives. Escape dismisses. Results render inline below the input in the ResultCanvas. Recent actions accessible via Cmd+Shift+K which opens the ActionHistoryDrawer from the bottom. The existing command palette keybind (Cmd+K) stays — the UI just becomes smarter.
mobile_adaptation: UniversalInput activates with a tap on a floating action button. IntentPreview appears as a bottom sheet. Context hints shown as horizontal chip scroll. Results push content down (inline, not overlay).
key_technical_risks: Intent parsing latency: AI server round-trip could make typing feel sluggish. Mitigation: client-side trie-based fuzzy matching for known commands (instant), server-side for novel intents (show skeleton spinner).

ResultCanvas rendering: different result types need different layouts. Mitigation: use Dynamic component from solid-js/web (already in codebase) with a ResultRenderer registry matching the DiffChanges/FileIcon pattern.
Command discovery: users won't know what's possible if there's no list. Mitigation: ContextHintBar serves as ambient discovery — shows relevant suggestions based on current page and recent actions.

vision_tagline: The app that watches your code while you write it — and tells you what matters
user_story: A developer is reviewing a PR on GitHub. OpenCode Desktop sits in the macOS menu bar (or Windows tray). It silently monitors git activity, CI status, and code complexity metrics. A subtle badge appears on the tray icon: '3 insights'. The developer clicks it, and a compact popover slides down: 'Test coverage in auth module dropped 12% (2 commits ago)', 'Function validateToken() has 47 lines — it was 28 when you last touched it', 'PR #142 has failing checks for 3 hours'. The developer clicks 'Show me' on the coverage insight. OpenCode opens the coverage report, highlights the affected files, and offers: 'Want me to write tests for the uncovered branches?'. Later, the developer is pushing a change that touches a file 5 other sessions modified this week. A subtle toast appears: 'Heavy contention on user.ts — 5 sessions touched it today. Consider splitting into smaller files.' The developer isn't interrupted — they're informed. The app learns what matters to them and quiets down when insights are repetitive.
core_innovation: Proactive, permission-asking background agent that monitors git/CI/code health and surfaces insights with zero friction. Not a dashboard you check — a lens you glance at. Insights are push-based but deferential: they accumulate silently and reveal on demand.

components.name: AmbientTray
components.description: macOS menu bar / Windows tray icon that shows a badge counter for actionable insights. Clicking opens the InsightDrawer popover. Right-click shows quick actions: 'Summarize today', 'Check all PRs', 'Dim until tomorrow'. The icon changes color based on urgency: default = idle, yellow = insights available, red = blocking issue detected.

components.name: InsightDrawer
components.description: Popover shown when clicking AmbientTray. Lists insights grouped by category (Code Health, CI/CD, Git Activity, Session Memory). Each insight is a compact card with icon, one-line summary, confidence indicator, and a 'Show me' / 'Dismiss' action. Supports marking insights as 'read' (they dim and move to bottom). Top insight is expanded by default with a preview.

components.name: InsightToast
components.description: Non-blocking toast that appears for high-urgency insights (blocking CI, security vulnerability). Uses the existing Toast system but with a 'soft' expansion: shows 1-line summary, auto-dismisses after 8 seconds, expands to full insight on hover. Never interrupts typing.

components.name: InsightDetailPane
components.description: Full-panel view opened when user clicks 'Show me' on an insight. Renders the detailed context: for a coverage drop, shows the coverage diff heatmap. For a complexity warning, shows the function with cyclomatic complexity annotations. For a PR alert, shows the PR diff summary. Each detail pane has a call-to-action button ('Write tests', 'Refactor', 'Review PR').

components.name: InsightSettingsPanel
components.description: Settings section where users configure what the ambient system monitors and how loudly it notifies. Categories: Git monitoring (branches, PRs), CI status, Code health (complexity thresholds, file size warnings), Session correlation (contention detection, cross-session pattern recognition). Per-category quiet hours and notification frequency sliders.

data_models.name: AmbientInsight
data_models.description: A single insight generated by the background monitoring system.

schema.category: coverage | complexity | git | ci | contention | security | session_memory
schema.source: string (file path, PR URL, test name)
schema.payload: Record<string, unknown> (context for the detail pane)

data_models.name: GitSnapshot
data_models.description: Periodic snapshot of git state used for diff analysis.

schema.complexity_deltas: Record<string, { lines_before: number; lines_after: number; complexity_estimate: number }>

integration_points.description: Add tray creation in the main Electron process. Register global shortcuts for quick-insight access. The tray process runs on a 5-minute interval calling GitSnapshot and CI status checks.
Add IPC handlers: 'get-ambient-insights', 'dismiss-insight', 'check-git-snapshot', 'check-ci-status'. These bridge between the main-process tray monitor and the renderer-process UI.
Add ambient insight API methods to the ElectronAPI bridge: getAmbientInsights, dismissInsight, getGitSnapshot.

Extend the Notification type with AmbientInsight variant. The existing notification system (append, lookup, dismiss patterns) already supports level-based notifications — ambient insights map to a new 'insight' discriminator.
Add ambient monitoring config block to the Settings interface. Use the existing persisted() pattern for config persistence.
Add AmbientProvider wrapping the app tree. Mounts an Effect-based interval that polls for new insights from the main process.
Add ambient insight badge to the titlebar. The existing badge infrastructure (session count, notification count) extends to show insight count.

editor_experience: The AmbientTray sits in the OS menu bar/tray. No window needs to be open. Click it → InsightDrawer drops down from the tray. Each insight is expandable. Click 'Show me' → the app window opens (if closed) or navigates to the InsightDetailPane showing the full context. Urgent insights (blocking CI, security) trigger a gentle Toast even when the app is in the foreground. The user can configure everything in Settings → Ambient. The ambient system learns: if the user always dismisses 'coverage drop' insights, their urgency lowers automatically.
mobile_adaptation: No persistent tray on mobile. Ambient insights are delivered as push notifications when the app is backgrounded. Opening the notification opens the InsightDetailPane. Configuration is available in mobile Settings.
key_technical_risks: Git polling overhead: running git diff every 5 minutes could be slow on large repos. Mitigation: use git's --shortstat for quick diff, only compute full diffs when an insight is expanded.
CI API rate limits: polling GitHub/GitLab CI could hit rate limits. Mitigation: use webhooks when possible, fall back to polling with configurable interval. Cache CI status for 2 minutes.

False positives: ambient insights must be genuinely useful or users will disable them. Mitigation: confidence scoring system — only surface insights above 0.7 confidence. Track user dismiss/reaction patterns to train the scoring.
